message.content:
Rewrite the file `docs/development.md` to match the current JavaFX 3.0.0-alpha.1 state.

Read the following files first to understand the current architecture:
- `/Users/phoebej/Develop/Java/SwissKitJ/CLAUDE.md` (especially "Plugin Development" and "Reusable UI Component: StepWizard" sections)
- `/Users/phoebej/Develop/Java/SwissKitJ/docs/development.md` (current version to understand structure)

Key changes from Swing to JavaFX:
- Plugin interface is `SwissKitJPlugin` (not `KitPage`), in `fan.summer.api` package
- No `@SwissKitPage` annotation — built-in tools use `BuiltinToolRegistrar`, external plugins use SPI
- Plugins return JavaFX `Node` via `createView()`, not Swing `JPanel` via `getPanel()`
- No SwingWorker — use JavaFX `Task` or `Platform.runLater()`
- No `SwingUtilities.invokeLater()` — use `Platform.runLater()`
- No `JOptionPane` — use JavaFX `Alert`
- Font is set via CSS, not `new Font("SansSerif", ...)`
- StepWizard is the reusable multi-step wizard component in `SwissKitJ-Api`
- Plugin logging via `fan.summer.api.log.LoggerFactory`
- Build requires JDK 21, entry point is `fan.summer.Launcher`
- `SwissKitJPlugin` methods: `getId()`, `getName()`, `getDescription()`, `getCategory()`, `getVersion()`, `getIconText()`, `getIconStyle()`, `getType()`, `createView()`, plus lifecycle methods `onActivate()`, `onDeactivate()`, `onUnload()`
- Category enum values: `TEXT`, `IMAGE`, `DEV`, `NET`, `OTHER`
- IconStyle enum values: `IC_BLUE`, `IC_PURPLE`, `IC_TEAL`, `IC_AMBER`, `IC_RED`, `IC_PINK`, `IC_GRAY`

Write the file completely. Remove all Swing-era content. Keep good structure for a development guide.

- docs-updater: Docs Updater Agent
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.

- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Development Guide
2	
3	This guide covers everything you need to know to contribute to SwissKit, from setting up your development environment to
4	implementing new features.
5	
6	## Table of Contents
7	
8	- [Prerequisites](#prerequisites)
9	- [Setting Up Development Environment](#setting-up-development-environment)
10	- [Project Structure](#project-structure)
11	- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
12	- [Adding New Tools](#adding-new-tools)
13	- [Testing](#testing)
14	- [Building](#building)
15	- [Common Tasks](#common-tasks)
16	
17	## Prerequisites
18	
19	Before you start developing, ensure you have:
20	
21	- **JDK 11 or higher**
22	- **Maven 3.6 or higher**
23	- **IntelliJ IDEA** (recommended) or your preferred IDE
24	- **Git**
25	
26	### Verify Installation
27	
28	```bash
29	# Check Java version
30	java -version
31	
32	# Check Maven version
33	mvn -version
34	
35	# Check Git version
36	git --version
37	```
38	
39	## Setting Up Development Environment
40	
41	### 1. Clone the Repository
42	
43	```bash
44	git clone https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ.git
45	cd SwissKit
46	```
47	
48	### 2. Import into IDE
49	
50	**IntelliJ IDEA**:
51	
52	1. Open IntelliJ IDEA
53	2. Select "Open" and choose the project directory
54	3. Wait for Maven to download dependencies
55	4. The project will be automatically recognized as a Maven project
56	
57	### 3. Build the Project
58	
59	```bash
60	mvn clean compile
61	```
62	
63	### 4. Run the Application
64	
65	```bash
66	mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
67	```
68	
69	Or run `Main.java` directly from your IDE.
70	
71	## Project Structure
72	
73	```
74	SwissKit/
75	├── src/main/java/fan/summer/
76	│   ├── Main.java                    # Entry point
77	│   ├── annoattion/                  # Annotations
78	│   │   └── SwissKitPage.java       # Page annotation
79	│   ├── kitpage/                     # Tool modules
80	│   │   ├── KitPage.java            # Interface
81	│   │   ├── KitPageScanner.java     # Auto-discovery scanner
82	│   │   ├── WelcomePage.java        # Welcome page
83	│   │   ├── excel/                  # Excel tool
84	│   │   │   ├── listener/           # Event listeners
85	│   │   │   └── plugin.swisskit.hpl.worker/             # Background workers
86	│   │   └── email/                  # Email tool
87	│   ├── plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui/                         # Custom UI components
88	│   │   └── components/
89	│   └── utils/                      # Utilities
90	├── src/main/resources/              # Resources (icons, etc.)
91	├── pom.xml                          # Maven configuration
92	└── docs/                            # Documentation
93	```
94	
95	## Code Standards
96	
97	### Naming Conventions
98	
99	**Packages**:
100	
101	- Tool pages: `fan.summer.kitpage.{toolName}`
102	- Listeners: `listener/{function}Listener.java`
103	- Workers: `plugin.swisskit.hpl.worker/{function}Worker.java`
104	- Callbacks: `plugin.swisskit.hpl.worker/{function}Callback.java`
105	
106	**Classes**:
107	
108	- Pages: `{ToolName}KitPage` or `{ToolName}Page`
109	- Listeners: `{Function}Listener`
110	- Workers: `{Function}Worker`
111	- Callbacks: `{Function}Callback`
112	
113	**Methods and Variables**:
114	
115	- Use camelCase
116	- Be descriptive and concise
117	
118	```java
119	// Good
120	public void analyzeExcelFile(Path filePath) { }
121	private int currentProgress;
122	
123	// Bad
124	public void analyzeExcel() { }
125	private int cp;
126	```
127	
128	### Documentation
129	
130	- Add Javadoc for all public methods
131	- Use clear, concise descriptions
132	- Include parameters and return values
133	
134	```java
135	/**
136	 * Analyzes an Excel file and extracts header information.
137	 *
138	 * @param filePath the path to the Excel file to analyze
139	 * @return a map of sheet names to their headers
140	 * @throws IOException if the file cannot be read
141	 */
142	public Map<String, Map<Integer, String>> analyzeExcel(Path filePath) throws IOException {
143	    // Implementation
144	}
145	```
146	
147	### Language
148	
149	- All code comments must be in **English**
150	- All UI text must be in **English**
151	- Variable and method names in English
152	
153	### UI Standards
154	
155	- Use `SansSerif` font for UI components
156	- Use color constants from `UIUtils`
157	- Recommend layouts: `BorderLayout`, `GridBagLayout`, `BoxLayout`
158	
159	```java
160	JLabel titleLabel = new JLabel("My Tool");
161	titleLabel.setFont(new Font("SansSerif", Font.BOLD, 18));
162	panel.add(titleLabel, BorderLayout.NORTH);
163	```
164	
165	## Adding New Tools
166	
167	### Step-by-Step Guide
168	
169	**1. Create Package Structure**
170	
171	```bash
172	mkdir -p src/main/java/fan/summer/kitpage/mytool
173	```
174	
175	**2. Implement KitPage Interface with @SwissKitPage Annotation**
176	
177	```java
178	package fan.summer.kitpage.mytool;
179	
180	import fan.summer.api.KitPage;
181	import fan.summer.annoattion.SwissKitPage;
182	
183	import javax.swing.*;
184	import java.awt.*;
185	
186	@SwissKitPage(
187	        menuName = "🔧 My Tool",
188	        menuTooltip = "Open My Tool",
189	        visible = true,
190	        order = 10
191	)
192	public class MyToolPage implements KitPage {
193	    private JPanel panel;
194	
195	    public MyToolPage() {
196	        initComponents();
197	    }
198	
199	    private void initComponents() {
200	        panel = new JPanel(new BorderLayout());
201	
202	        // Add your UI components here
203	        JLabel titleLabel = new JLabel("My Tool");
204	        titleLabel.setFont(new Font("SansSerif", Font.BOLD, 18));
205	
206	        panel.add(titleLabel, BorderLayout.NORTH);
207	    }
208	
209	    @Override
210	    public JPanel getPanel() {
211	        return panel;
212	    }
213	}
214	```
215	
216	**Annotation Properties**:
217	
218	| Property      | Type    | Default | Description                   |
219	|---------------|---------|---------|-------------------------------|
220	| `menuName`    | String  | ""      | Display name in sidebar       |
221	| `menuTooltip` | String  | ""      | Tooltip on hover              |
222	| `visible`     | boolean | true    | Whether to show in menu       |
223	| `order`       | int     | 0       | Display order (lower = first) |
224	
225	**3. Build and Test**
226	
227	```bash
228	mvn clean compile
229	mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
230	```
231	
232	Your new tool will appear automatically in the sidebar!
233	
234	### Adding Background Processing
235	
236	For time-consuming operations, use SwingWorker:
237	
238	```java
239	public class MyToolPage implements KitPage {
240	    private JProgressBar progressBar;
241	    private JButton processButton;
242	
243	    public MyToolPage() {
244	        initComponents();
245	    }
246	
247	    private void initComponents() {
248	        panel = new JPanel(new BorderLayout());
249	
250	        // Create components
251	        progressBar = new GradientProgressBar();
252	        processButton = new JButton("Process");
253	
254	        // Add action listener
255	        processButton.addActionListener(e -> {
256	            new MyWorker(progressBar, processButton, panel).execute();
257	        });
258	
259	        // Layout
260	        panel.add(progressBar, BorderLayout.CENTER);
261	        panel.add(processButton, BorderLayout.SOUTH);
262	    }
263	}
264	
265	class MyWorker extends SwingWorker<Void, Integer> {
266	    private final JProgressBar progressBar;
267	    private final JButton button;
268	    private final Component parent;
269	
270	    public MyWorker(JProgressBar progressBar, JButton button, Component parent) {
271	        this.progressBar = progressBar;
272	        this.button = button;
273	        this.parent = parent;
274	    }
275	
276	    @Override
277	    protected Void doInBackground() throws Exception {
278	        SwingUtilities.invokeLater(() -> {
279	            progressBar.setValue(0);
280	            progressBar.setStringPainted(true);
281	            button.setEnabled(false);
282	        });
283	
284	        // Do work here
285	        for (int i = 0; i <= 100; i++) {
286	            Thread.sleep(50);
287	            publish(i);
288	        }
289	
290	        return null;
291	    }
292	
293	    @Override
294	    protected void process(List<Integer> chunks) {
295	        if (!chunks.isEmpty()) {
296	            int latest = chunks.get(chunks.size() - 1);
297	            progressBar.setValue(latest);
298	            progressBar.setString("Processing... " + latest + "%");
299	        }
300	    }
301	
302	    @Override
303	    protected void done() {
304	        try {
305	            get();
306	            progressBar.setValue(100);
307	            progressBar.setString("Completed!");
308	            button.setEnabled(true);
309	        } catch (Exception e) {
310	            progressBar.setString("Failed: " + e.getMessage());
311	            JOptionPane.showMessageDialog(parent, e.getMessage(), "Error", JOptionPane.ERROR_MESSAGE);
312	            button.setEnabled(true);
313	        }
314	    }
315	}
316	```
317	
318	## Testing
319	
320	### Running Tests
321	
322	```bash
323	mvn test
324	```
325	
326	### Writing Tests
327	
328	```java
329	import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
330	import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;
331	
332	class ExcelKitPageTest {
333	
334	    @Test
335	    void testGetTitle() {
336	        ExcelKitPage page = new ExcelKitPage();
337	        assertEquals("Excel", page.getTitle());
338	    }
339	}
340	```
341	
342	## Building
343	
344	### Clean Build
345	
346	```bash
347	mvn clean package
348	```
349	
350	### Skip Tests
351	
352	```bash
353	mvn clean package -DskipTests
354	```
355	
356	### Create Executable JAR
357	
358	```bash
359	mvn clean package
360	# Output: target/SwissKit-2.1.1.jar
361	```
362	
363	### Run Executable JAR
364	
365	```bash
366	java -jar target/SwissKit-2.1.1.jar
367	```
368	
369	## Common Tasks
370	
371	### Adding a Dependency
372	
373	Edit `pom.xml`:
374	
375	```xml
376	<dependency>
377	    <groupId>com.example</groupId>
378	    <artifactId>library-name</artifactId>
379	    <version>1.0.0</version>
380	</dependency>
381	```
382	
383	Then run:
384	
385	```bash
386	mvn dependency:resolve
387	```
388	
389	### Adding an Icon
390	
391	Place icon in `src/main/resources/`:
392	
393	- `icon.png` (preferred)
394	- `icon.jpg`
395	- `app.png`
396	
397	### Debugging
398	
399	Enable verbose logging:
400	
401	```bash
402	java -Dlog4j.configurationFile=path/to/log4j2.xml -jar target/SwissKit-1.1.0.jar
403	```
404	
405	Or add logging to your code:
406	
407	```java
408	private static final Logger logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MyClass.class);
409	
410	logger.info("Processing started");
411	logger.error("Error occurred", exception);
412	```
413	
414	## Best Practices
415	
416	### Thread Safety
417	
418	- Always update UI components in EDT thread
419	- Use `SwingUtilities.invokeLater()` for UI updates from background threads
420	
421	```java
422	// Wrong - from background thread
423	progressBar.setValue(50);
424	
425	// Correct
426	SwingUtilities.invokeLater(() -> progressBar.setValue(50));
427	```
428	
429	### Error Handling
430	
431	- Always handle exceptions gracefully
432	- Provide user-friendly error messages
433	- Log errors for debugging
434	
435	```java
436	try {
437	    // Operation
438	} catch (Exception e) {
439	    logger.error("Operation failed", e);
440	    JOptionPane.showMessageDialog(parent,
441	            "Operation failed: " + e.getMessage(),
442	            "Error", JOptionPane.ERROR_MESSAGE);
443	}
444	```
445	
446	### Resource Management
447	
448	- Use try-with-resources for file operations
449	- Close resources properly
450	
451	```java
452	try (Workbook workbook = WorkbookFactory.create(file)) {
453	    // Use workbook
454	} // Automatically closed
455	```
456	
457	## Contributing
458	
459	See the [Contributing Guide](contributing.md) for guidelines on contributing to SwissKit.
460	
461	---
462	
463	**Ready to contribute?** Check out [Contributing](contributing.md) for submission guidelines!

1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## Build & Run
6	
7	**Build order is required** — the API module must be installed before anything else compiles:
8	
9	```bash
10	# Step 1: Install the API module
11	mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests
12	
13	# Step 2: Build the full project (from repo root)
14	mvn clean package -DskipTests
15	
16	# Run the application
17	java -jar SwissKit/target/SwissKit-2.1.1.jar
18	```
19	
20	To build and run only the main app module:
21	```bash
22	mvn clean package -pl SwissKit -am -DskipTests
23	```
24	
25	On Windows, the `windows-exe` Maven profile is auto-activated and produces `SwissKit.exe` via Launch4j. GitHub Actions handles multi-platform builds — local Maven is not required for releases.
26	
27	## Module Structure
28	
29	| Module | Purpose |
30	|--------|---------|
31	| `SwissKitJ-Api` | Shared plugin interface + reusable UI components (`SwissKitJPlugin`, `StepWizard`) |
32	| `SwissKit` | Main JavaFX application — UI shell, plugin loading, built-in tools |
33	| `OfficalPlugin/SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning` | Auto-learning plugin |
34	| `OfficalPlugin/SwissKitJ-Plugin-Qcc` | CSV-to-Excel converter plugin |
35	| `OfficalPlugin/SwissKit-Plugin-Mouse` | Mouse automation plugin |
36	
37	All plugins declare `SwissKitJ-Api` as `provided` scope. The main app provides it at runtime via the fat JAR.
38	
39	## Architecture
40	
41	**Entry point**: `fan.summer.Launcher` (fat-JAR manifest) → `fan.summer.app.SwissKitJApp` (JavaFX `Application`).
42	
43	**Startup sequence** (in `SwissKitJApp.start()`):
44	1. Resolve `plugins/` directory (JAR sibling in production, `./plugins/` in dev)
45	2. Create `PluginLoader` + `PluginRegistry`
46	3. Register built-in tools via `BuiltinToolRegistrar` (bypasses JAR loading, directly adds to registry)
47	4. Build `MainWindow` and display it
48	5. Start `PluginLoader` (scans `plugins/` dir and watches for changes)
49	
50	**UI structure** (all in `fan.summer.ui.*`):
51	- `MainWindow` — root `StackPane`; owns `TitleBar`, `Sidebar`, `ContentArea`, status bar
52	- `Sidebar` — category-based navigation; categories are `all / text / image / dev / net / other`
53	- `ContentArea` — shows `ToolCard` grid or active tool view; manages `DetailPanel` and the back-bar for returning from a tool
54	- `DetailPanel` — slide-in panel showing plugin metadata; has a Launch button that fires `onLaunch`
55	- `TitleBar` — custom window chrome (window is `StageStyle.TRANSPARENT`)
56	
57	**Navigation flow**: `ToolCard` click → `DetailPanel.show()` → Launch button → `MainWindow.wireEvents` callback → `registry.activate(plugin)` + `contentArea.showPage(plugin.createView(), title)`. The back bar (shown by `ContentArea`) calls `registry.deactivate()` on return.
58	
59	**Theming**: Three-layer CSS structure (glassmorphism dark theme).
60	
61	| File | Module | Scope |
62	|---|---|---|
63	| `css/swisskit-common.css` | `SwissKitJ-Api` | Shared variables, scrollbars, progress bar, `.glass-*` utility classes (dialog/field/tab-pane/combo/table/checkbox/btn-primary/btn-secondary), `.section-title`/`.section-header`. Loaded into the main Scene + available to any third-party plugin. |
64	| `css/shell.css` | `SwissKit` | App-shell only — `.titlebar`, `.sidebar`, `.search-bar`, `.tool-card`, `.detail-panel`, `.statusbar`. Loaded into the main Scene by `SwissKitJApp`. |
65	| `css/builtin.css` | `SwissKit` | Reserved for built-in tool styling. Currently empty placeholder. |
66	
67	Plugins embedded in the main Scene (the normal `createView()` flow) automatically inherit all three stylesheets via scene graph propagation — no action needed. Plugins that open their own `Stage`/`Scene` should call `fan.summer.api.theme.Themes.applyTo(scene)` to get the common utility classes.
68	
69	Plugin icon background colors are CSS classes: `ic-blue / ic-purple / ic-teal / ic-amber / ic-red / ic-pink / ic-gray` (declared in `shell.css`; actual color injection happens in Java via `DropShadow` per `IconStyle`).
70	
71	**Database**: H2 file at `.swisskit/swisskit.db` relative to the runtime working directory. Schema initialized from `init.sql`. Accessed via MyBatis; mapper XMLs are in `src/main/resources/mapper/`.
72	
73	**i18n**: `src/main/resources/i18n/messages.properties` (Chinese default), `messages_en.properties` (English).
74	
75	## Reusable UI Component: StepWizard
76	
77	`fan.summer.api.component.StepWizard` (in `SwissKitJ-Api`) is a ready-made multi-step wizard container for use inside any plugin's `createView()`.
78	
79	```java
80	StepWizard wizard = new StepWizard();
81	wizard.addStep("Select file",  step1Node, () -> filePath != null);
82	wizard.addStep("Split mode",   step2Node, () -> modeSelected);
83	wizard.addStep("Output path",  step3Node, () -> outputPath != null);
84	wizard.build();   // must call after all addStep() calls
85	
86	// Intercept step transitions (e.g., trigger async work when moving from step 0 → 1):
87	wizard.setOnStepChanged((from, to, total) -> {
88	    if (from == 0 && to == 1) startAnalysis();
89	});
90	
91	// Programmatic navigation:
92	wizard.goTo(2);
93	boolean last = wizard.isLastStep();
94	```
95	
96	The wizard renders step dots with done/active/idle states, animated slide transitions between steps, and Back/Next buttons. The `canProceed` supplier is evaluated on every Next click — return `false` to trigger a shake animation and block advancement.
97	
98	## Plugin Development
99	
100	**Interface**: `fan.summer.api.SwissKitJPlugin` (in `SwissKitJ-Api`)
101	
102	```java
103	public interface SwissKitJPlugin {
104	    String getId();          // reverse-domain ID, e.g. "com.example.my-tool"
105	    String getName();
106	    String getDescription();
107	    String getCategory();    // dev / text / image / net / other
108	    String getVersion();
109	    String getIconText();    // emoji or single char
110	    default String getIconStyle() { return "ic-blue"; }  // CSS class for icon bg
111	    default String getType()      { return "plugin"; }   // "builtin" for built-ins
112	
113	    Node createView();       // called once; result cached and reused
114	    default void onActivate()   {}
115	    default void onDeactivate() {}
116	    default void onUnload()     {}
117	}
118	```
119	
120	**External plugins** (JAR-based):
121	1. Implement `SwissKitJPlugin`
122	2. Declare in `META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.SwissKitJPlugin`
123	3. Drop JAR into `plugins/` directory; hot-reload is supported
124	
125	**Built-in tools** skip SPI entirely — `BuiltinToolRegistrar.register()` adds them directly to `PluginRegistry`. See existing tools there as templates.
126	
127	### Plugin logging
128	
129	Plugins should use `fan.summer.api.log.LoggerFactory` (in `SwissKitJ-Api`) rather than depending on SLF4J directly. The host installs a binder at startup that routes plugin log calls into the same SLF4J + Logback backbone used by the host (console at INFO+, rolling file at DEBUG+ under `.swisskit/logs/swisskit.log`, daily rotation, 7-day retention).
130	
131	```java
132	import fan.summer.api.log.LoggerFactory;
133	import fan.summer.api.log.PluginLogger;
134	
135	public class MyPlugin implements SwissKitJPlugin {
136	    private static final PluginLogger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MyPlugin.class);
137	
138	    @Override public void onActivate() {
139	        log.info("Activated, taskId={}", currentTaskId);
140	    }
141	}
142	```
143	
144	Use SLF4J-style `{}` placeholders — formatting is deferred until the level is actually enabled. If the host has not installed a binder (e.g. plugin unit tests), `LoggerFactory` returns a silent no-op logger, so it is safe to call from anywhere.
145	
146	## Branch Status — v3.0.0-JavaFX
147	
148	This branch is an active migration from Swing/FlatLaf to JavaFX. Legacy Swing classes live in `backup/SwissKit/` and `backup/SwissKitJ-Api/` under the project root, and are **excluded from Maven compilation** via `<excludes>` in `SwissKit/pom.xml`. Do not move files out of `backup/` unless completing their JavaFX port.
149	
150	The plugin interface was also renamed: the old `fan.summer.api.KitPage` (Swing `JPanel`-based) is replaced by `fan.summer.api.SwissKitJPlugin` (JavaFX `Node`-based).
151	
152	## Excel Splitter — Porting Reference
153	
154	The backup Swing implementation at `backup/SwissKit/java/fan/summer/kitpage/excel/` is the authoritative reference for the Excel split logic. Key classes:
155	
156	| Backup class | Role |
157	|---|---|
158	| `ExcelKitPage` | Top-level Swing page (UI only — replace with JavaFX + StepWizard) |
159	| `ExcelAnalysisWorker` | Reads all sheets + row-0 headers via Apache POI → `Map<String, Map<Integer, String>>` |
160	| `ExcelSplitWorker` | Three split modes — see below |
161	| `NoModelDataListener` | Apache Fesod `AnalysisEventListener` that caches rows as `List<Map<Integer,Object>>` |
162	| `ExcelUtil` | POI helpers: `appendSheet`, `appendDataRowsByPoi`, `copyEntireSheet`, `normalizeOrInvalid` |
163	| `FileNameUtil` | `getFileName(String)` — strips extension |
164	
165	**Three split modes** (set via `ExcelSplitWorker.setXxxModel()`):
166	
167	| Mode key | Method | What it does |
168	|---|---|---|
169	| `SSM` | `setSplitSheetModel(Set<String> sheets)` | One output file per selected sheet |
170	| `SCM` | `setSplitColumnModel(String sheet, String column)` | Groups rows by unique column value → one file per value |
171	| `SCPM` | `setComplexSplitModel(String taskId)` | Multi-config: reads `ComplexSplitConfigEntity` rows from H2; supports normal split + copy-all (headerIndex==-1 && columnIndex==-1) |
172	
173	**Analysis result map shape**: `Map<sheetName, Map<columnIndex, columnHeader>>` — the outer key is sheet name (insertion order preserved via `LinkedHashMap`), the inner key is the zero-based column index from POI.
174	
175	**Fesod library** (`org.apache.fesod:fesod-sheet`) is used for high-throughput reading/writing in split operations. Core pattern:
176	```java
177	// Read
178	NoModelDataListener listener = new NoModelDataListener();
179	try (ExcelReader reader = FesodSheet.read(file).build()) {
180	    ReadSheet sheet = FesodSheet.readSheet(sheetName)
181	        .headRowNumber(headerRowIndex)
182	        .registerReadListener(listener).build();
183	    reader.read(sheet);
184	}
185	List<Map<Integer, Object>> rows = listener.getCachedDataList();
186	
187	// Write
188	FesodSheet.write(outputFile)
189	    .sheet(sheetName)
190	    .head(buildHeaders(headerMap))   // List<List<String>>
191	    .doWrite(buildRows(headerMap, rows));  // List<List<Object>>
192	```
193	
194	**Complex split DB entity**: `ComplexSplitConfigEntity` fields — `taskId`, `fieldName` (original filename), `sheetName`, `headerIndex` (1-based row of headers), `columnIndex` (1-based column to split by). A row with `headerIndex == -1 && columnIndex == -1` means "copy entire sheet to all output files".
195	
196	## Custom Commands
197	
198	- `/docs-updater` — Updates version numbers and content across `docs/`, `README.md`, and `CHANGELOG.md` to match current pom.xml versions.
199	- `/release <version>` — Full release workflow: bumps all pom.xml versions, updates `CHANGELOG.md`, commits, creates and pushes a `v{version}` git tag, which triggers GitHub Actions for multi-platform builds.
200	
201	## Commit Convention
202	
203	Follow conventional commits with emojis:
204	- `✨` / `:sparkles:` — new feature
205	- `🐛` / `:bug:` — bug fix
206	- `♻️` / `:recycle:` — refactor
207	- `📝` / `:memo:` — documentation
208	- `⬆️` / `:arrow_up:` — dependency upgrade
209

content.content:
1	# Development Guide
2	
3	This guide covers everything you need to know to contribute to SwissKit, from setting up your development environment to building plugins and built-in tools.
4	
5	## Table of Contents
6	
7	- [Prerequisites](#prerequisites)
8	- [Setting Up Development Environment](#setting-up-development-environment)
9	- [Project Structure](#project-structure)
10	- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
11	- [Plugin Development](#plugin-development)
12	- [Built-in Tools](#built-in-tools)
13	- [Background Processing](#background-processing)
14	- [UI Components](#ui-components)
15	- [Theming](#theming)
16	- [Logging](#logging)
17	- [Testing](#testing)
18	- [Building](#building)
19	- [Common Tasks](#common-tasks)
20	
21	## Prerequisites
22	
23	Before you start developing, ensure you have:
24	
25	- **JDK 21 or higher** (required for JavaFX 21+)
26	- **Maven 3.8 or higher**
27	- **IntelliJ IDEA** (recommended) or your preferred IDE
28	- **Git**
29	
30	### Verify Installation
31	
32	```bash
33	# Check Java version
34	java -version
35	
36	# Check Maven version
37	mvn -version
38	
39	# Check Git version
40	git --version
41	```
42	
43	## Setting Up Development Environment
44	
45	### 1. Clone the Repository
46	
47	```bash
48	git clone https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ.git
49	cd SwissKitJ
50	```
51	
52	### 2. Import into IDE
53	
54	**IntelliJ IDEA**:
55	
56	1. Open IntelliJ IDEA
57	2. Select "Open" and choose the project directory
58	3. Wait for Maven to download dependencies
59	4. The project will be automatically recognized as a multi-module Maven project
60	
61	### 3. Build the Project
62	
63	The API module must be installed first before anything else compiles:
64	
65	```bash
66	# Step 1: Install the API module
67	mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests
68	
69	# Step 2: Build the full project
70	mvn clean package -DskipTests
71	```
72	
73	To build only the main app module and its dependencies:
74	
75	```bash
76	mvn clean package -pl SwissKit -am -DskipTests
77	```
78	
79	### 4. Run the Application
80	
81	```bash
82	java -jar SwissKit/target/SwissKit-3.0.0-alpha.1.jar
83	```
84	
85	Or run `fan.summer.Launcher` directly from your IDE.
86	
87	## Project Structure
88	
89	```
90	SwissKitJ/
91	├── SwissKitJ-Api/                        # Shared API module
92	│   └── src/main/java/fan/summer/api/
93	│       ├── SwissKitJPlugin.java          # Plugin interface
94	│       ├── ToolCategory.java             # Category enum
95	│       ├── IconStyle.java                # Icon style enum
96	│       ├── ToolType.java                 # Type enum (BUILTIN / PLUGIN)
97	│       ├── component/
98	│       │   └── StepWizard.java           # Reusable multi-step wizard
99	│       ├── log/
100	│       │   ├── LoggerFactory.java        # Plugin logger entry point
101	│       │   └── PluginLogger.java         # Logger interface
102	│       └── theme/
103	│           └── Themes.java               # Theme utility (CSS loading)
104	├── SwissKit/                             # Main JavaFX application
105	│   └── src/main/java/fan/summer/
106	│       ├── Launcher.java                 # Entry point (fat JAR)
107	│       ├── app/
108	│       │   └── SwissKitJApp.java         # JavaFX Application
109	│       ├── buildintool/                  # Built-in tool implementations
110	│       │   ├── dev/                      #   Base64, Hash, JSON
111	│       │   ├── email/                    #   Email sender
112	│       │   ├── excelsplitter/            #   Excel splitter
113	│       │   ├── image/                    #   Color converter
114	│       │   └── text/                     #   Markdown editor
115	│       ├── plugin/                       # Plugin loading and registry
116	│       ├── ui/                           # App shell UI (MainWindow, Sidebar, etc.)
117	│       ├── Registrar/
118	│       │   └── BuiltinToolRegistrar.java # Registers built-in tools
119	│       └── util/                         # Utilities
120	├── OfficalPlugin/                        # Official external plugins
121	│   ├── SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning/   #   Auto-learning plugin
122	│   ├── SwissKitJ-Plugin-Qcc/             #   CSV-to-Excel converter
123	│   └── SwissKit-Plugin-Mouse/            #   Mouse automation
124	├── backup/                               # Legacy Swing code (excluded from build)
125	├── docs/                                 # Documentation
126	└── pom.xml                               # Root Maven configuration
127	```
128	
129	### Module Dependencies
130	
131	| Module | Depends on | Scope |
132	|--------|-----------|-------|
133	| `SwissKitJ-Api` | JavaFX | compile |
134	| `SwissKit` | `SwissKitJ-Api` | compile |
135	| `OfficalPlugin/*` | `SwissKitJ-Api` | provided |
136	
137	All plugins declare `SwissKitJ-Api` as `provided` scope. The main application provides it at runtime via the fat JAR.
138	
139	### Key Packages
140	
141	| Package | Purpose |
142	|---------|---------|
143	| `fan.summer.api` | Plugin interface and shared enums |
144	| `fan.summer.api.component` | Reusable UI components (StepWizard) |
145	| `fan.summer.api.log` | Plugin logging API |
146	| `fan.summer.api.theme` | Theme utilities for standalone plugin windows |
147	| `fan.summer.ui` | Application shell: MainWindow, Sidebar, ContentArea, TitleBar |
148	| `fan.summer.plugin` | Plugin discovery, loading, and registry |
149	| `fan.summer.buildintool.*` | Built-in tool implementations |
150	| `fan.summer.app` | JavaFX Application subclass |
151	
152	## Code Standards
153	
154	### Naming Conventions
155	
156	**Packages**:
157	
158	- Built-in tools: `fan.summer.buildintool.{category}`
159	- External plugins: `plugin.{org}.{toolname}` (e.g., `plugin.swisskitj`)
160	- Listeners and workers: use descriptive sub-packages
161	
162	**Classes**:
163	
164	- Plugin implementations: `{ToolName}Plugin`
165	- Built-in tools: `{ToolName}Plugin` under `fan.summer.buildintool.{category}`
166	- Utilities: `{Function}Util`
167	
168	**Methods and Variables**:
169	
170	- Use camelCase
171	- Be descriptive and concise
172	
173	```java
174	// Good
175	public void analyzeExcelFile(Path filePath) { }
176	private int currentProgress;
177	
178	// Bad
179	public void analyzeExcel() { }
180	private int cp;
181	```
182	
183	### Documentation
184	
185	- Add Javadoc for all public methods
186	- Use clear, concise descriptions
187	- Include parameters and return values
188	
189	```java
190	/**
191	 * Analyzes an Excel file and extracts header information.
192	 *
193	 * @param filePath the path to the Excel file to analyze
194	 * @return a map of sheet names to their headers
195	 * @throws IOException if the file cannot be read
196	 */
197	public Map<String, Map<Integer, String>> analyzeExcel(Path filePath) throws IOException {
198	    // Implementation
199	}
200	```
201	
202	### Language
203	
204	- All code comments must be in **English**
205	- All UI text must be in **English**
206	- Variable and method names in English
207	
208	### UI Standards
209	
210	- Use JavaFX layouts: `VBox`, `HBox`, `GridPane`, `BorderPane`, `StackPane`
211	- Font styling via CSS, not inline `setFont()` calls
212	- Use the CSS class constants in `IconStyle` for icon backgrounds
213	- Prefer `StepWizard` for multi-step workflows
214	
215	```java
216	VBox container = new VBox(16);
217	container.setPadding(new Insets(24));
218	container.setAlignment(Pos.TOP_LEFT);
219	
220	Label title = new Label("My Tool");
221	title.getStyleClass().add("section-title");
222	container.getChildren().add(title);
223	```
224	
225	## Plugin Development
226	
227	SwissKit supports two kinds of tools: **built-in tools** compiled directly into the application, and **external plugins** loaded at runtime from JAR files. Both implement the same `SwissKitJPlugin` interface.
228	
229	### The SwissKitJPlugin Interface
230	
231	```java
232	package fan.summer.api;
233	
234	import fan.summer.api.*;
235	import javafx.scene.Node;
236	
237	public interface SwissKitJPlugin {
238	    // ── Metadata (used by sidebar, search, and detail panel) ──
239	
240	    /** Globally unique ID; reverse-domain notation recommended: com.example.my-tool */
241	    String getId();
242	
243	    /** Display name shown on the tool card, e.g. "JSON Formatter" */
244	    String getName();
245	
246	    /** One-line description shown on the card and detail panel */
247	    String getDescription();
248	
249	    /** Category matching sidebar navigation */
250	    ToolCategory getCategory();
251	
252	    /** Version string, e.g. "1.0.0" */
253	    String getVersion();
254	
255	    /** Material Design Icons name (without "mdi" prefix), e.g. "file-excel" */
256	    String getMdiIcon();
257	
258	    /** CSS class and colour for the icon background */
259	    default IconStyle getIconStyle() { return IconStyle.BLUE; }
260	
261	    /** Type tag: BUILTIN for built-in tools, PLUGIN for external plugins */
262	    default ToolType getType() { return ToolType.PLUGIN; }
263	
264	    // ── UI lifecycle ──
265	
266	    /** Returns the main UI node for this tool. Called once; result is cached. */
267	    Node createView();
268	
269	    /** Called when the tool is brought to the foreground */
270	    default void onActivate() {}
271	
272	    /** Called when the tool is moved to the background */
273	    default void onDeactivate() {}
274	
275	    /** Called before the plugin is unloaded. Release resources here. */
276	    default void onUnload() {}
277	}
278	```
279	
280	### Category Enum
281	
282	```java
283	public enum ToolCategory {
284	    DEV,    // developer tools
285	    TEXT,   // text processing
286	    IMAGE,  // image processing
287	    NET,    // network tools
288	    OTHER   // miscellaneous
289	}
290	```
291	
292	### IconStyle Enum
293	
294	```java
295	public enum IconStyle {
296	    BLUE("ic-blue",   Color.rgb(99, 130, 255)),
297	    PURPLE("ic-purple", Color.rgb(160, 110, 255)),
298	    TEAL("ic-teal",   Color.rgb(40, 210, 140)),
299	    AMBER("ic-amber", Color.rgb(255, 185, 50)),
300	    RED("ic-red",    Color.rgb(255, 100, 100)),
301	    PINK("ic-pink",   Color.rgb(245, 100, 160)),
302	    GRAY("ic-gray",   Color.rgb(200, 200, 210));
303	}
304	```
305	
306	### Creating an External Plugin
307	
308	**1. Set up your Maven project**
309	
310	```xml
311	<dependencies>
312	    <dependency>
313	        <groupId>fan.summer.api</groupId>
314	        <artifactId>SwissKitJ-Api</artifactId>
315	        <version>3.0.0-alpha.1</version>
316	        <scope>provided</scope>
317	    </dependency>
318	</dependencies>
319	```
320	
321	**2. Implement the interface**
322	
323	```java
324	package plugin.swisskitj.mytool;
325	
326	import fan.summer.api.*;
327	import javafx.geometry.Insets;
328	import javafx.geometry.Pos;
329	import javafx.scene.Node;
330	import javafx.scene.control.Label;
331	import javafx.scene.layout.VBox;
332	
333	public class MyToolPlugin implements SwissKitJPlugin {
334	
335	    @Override
336	    public String getId()          { return "com.example.my-tool"; }
337	    @Override
338	    public String getName()        { return "My Tool"; }
339	    @Override
340	    public String getDescription() { return "Does something useful"; }
341	    @Override
342	    public ToolCategory getCategory() { return ToolCategory.DEV; }
343	    @Override
344	    public String getVersion()     { return "1.0.0"; }
345	    @Override
346	    public String getMdiIcon()     { return "wrench"; }
347	    @Override
348	    public IconStyle getIconStyle(){ return IconStyle.TEAL; }
349	
350	    @Override
351	    public Node createView() {
352	        VBox root = new VBox(16);
353	        root.setPadding(new Insets(24));
354	        root.setAlignment(Pos.TOP_LEFT);
355	
356	        Label title = new Label("My Tool");
357	        title.getStyleClass().add("section-title");
358	        root.getChildren().add(title);
359	
360	        return root;
361	    }
362	}
363	```
364	
365	**3. Register via SPI**
366	
367	Create `META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.SwissKitJPlugin` with the fully-qualified class name:
368	
369	```
370	plugin.swisskitj.mytool.MyToolPlugin
371	```
372	
373	**4. Package and deploy**
374	
375	Package your plugin as a fat JAR (include all private dependencies). Drop the JAR into the host application's `plugins/` directory. Hot-reload is supported -- the plugin loader watches the directory for changes.
376	
377	### Lifecycle Methods
378	
379	| Method | When Called | Typical Use |
380	|--------|-------------|-------------|
381	| `createView()` | First time the user launches the tool | Build and return the UI node. Called once, result cached. |
382	| `onActivate()` | Tool is brought to the foreground | Resume timers, refresh data, re-register listeners |
383	| `onDeactivate()` | Tool is moved to the background | Pause timers, persist state, unregister listeners |
384	| `onUnload()` | Plugin is being unloaded | Release threads, file handles, DB connections |
385	
386	### Navigation Flow
387	
388	1. User clicks a `ToolCard` in the sidebar grid
389	2. `DetailPanel` slides in showing plugin metadata (name, description, version, icon)
390	3. User clicks the Launch button
391	4. `registry.activate(plugin)` is called, triggering `onActivate()`
392	5. `contentArea.showPage(plugin.createView(), title)` embeds the tool's UI
393	6. When the user clicks the back bar, `registry.deactivate()` is called, triggering `onDeactivate()`
394	
395	## Built-in Tools
396	
397	Built-in tools skip SPI entirely. They are registered directly in `BuiltinToolRegistrar`.
398	
399	### Adding a Built-in Tool
400	
401	**1. Create the implementation class**
402	
403	```java
404	package fan.summer.buildintool.dev;
405	
406	import fan.summer.api.*;
407	import javafx.scene.Node;
408	
409	public class MyBuiltinPlugin implements SwissKitJPlugin {
410	
411	    @Override
412	    public String getId()          { return "builtin.my-tool"; }
413	    @Override
414	    public String getName()        { return "My Tool"; }
415	    @Override
416	    public String getDescription() { return "A built-in developer tool"; }
417	    @Override
418	    public ToolCategory getCategory() { return ToolCategory.DEV; }
419	    @Override
420	    public String getVersion()     { return "3.0.0-alpha.1"; }
421	    @Override
422	    public String getMdiIcon()     { return "wrench"; }
423	    @Override
424	    public IconStyle getIconStyle(){ return IconStyle.BLUE; }
425	    @Override
426	    public ToolType getType()      { return ToolType.BUILTIN; }
427	
428	    @Override
429	    public Node createView() {
430	        // Build and return the tool's UI
431	    }
432	}
433	```
434	
435	**2. Register in `BuiltinToolRegistrar`**
436	
437	```java
438	// In fan.summer.Registrar.BuiltinToolRegistrar.register()
439	List<SwissKitJPlugin> builtins = List.of(
440	    new JsonFormatterPlugin(),
441	    new Base64Plugin(),
442	    // ...
443	    new MyBuiltinPlugin()   // <-- Add here
444	);
445	```
446	
447	**3. Build and run**
448	
449	```bash
450	mvn clean package -pl SwissKit -am -DskipTests
451	java -jar SwissKit/target/SwissKit-3.0.0-alpha.1.jar
452	```
453	
454	## Background Processing
455	
456	For time-consuming operations, use JavaFX's `Task` class instead of `SwingWorker`.
457	
458	### JavaFX Task Pattern
459	
460	```java
461	import javafx.concurrent.Task;
462	import javafx.application.Platform;
463	
464	public class MyToolPlugin implements SwissKitJPlugin {
465	    private ProgressBar progressBar;
466	    private Button processButton;
467	
468	    private void startProcessing() {
469	        processButton.setDisable(true);
470	        progressBar.setProgress(0);
471	
472	        Task<Void> task = new Task<>() {
473	            @Override
474	            protected Void call() throws Exception {
475	                int totalSteps = 100;
476	                for (int i = 0; i <= totalSteps; i++) {
477	                    Thread.sleep(50);  // Simulate work
478	                    updateProgress(i, totalSteps);
479	                    updateMessage("Processing... " + i + "%");
480	                }
481	                return null;
482	            }
483	        };
484	
485	        // Bind progress to UI
486	        progressBar.progressProperty().bind(task.progressProperty());
487	
488	        task.setOnSucceeded(e -> {
489	            progressBar.setProgress(1);
490	            progressBar.progressProperty().unbind();
491	            processButton.setDisable(false);
492	        });
493	
494	        task.setOnFailed(e -> {
495	            progressBar.progressProperty().unbind();
496	            processButton.setDisable(false);
497	            Throwable ex = task.getException();
498	            showError(ex.getMessage());
499	        });
500	
501	        new Thread(task).start();
502	    }
503	}
504	```
505	
506	### Updating UI from Background Threads
507	
508	Use `Platform.runLater()` instead of `SwingUtilities.invokeLater()`:
509	
510	```java
511	// Correct: schedule UI update on the JavaFX Application Thread
512	Platform.runLater(() -> statusLabel.setText("Done"));
513	
514	// Wrong: calling UI methods from a background thread
515	statusLabel.setText("Done");  // Will throw IllegalStateException
516	```
517	
518	### Showing Dialogs
519	
520	Use JavaFX `Alert` instead of `JOptionPane`:
521	
522	```java
523	import javafx.scene.control.Alert;
524	import javafx.scene.control.Alert.AlertType;
525	
526	private void showError(String message) {
527	    Alert alert = new Alert(AlertType.ERROR);
528	    alert.setTitle("Error");
529	    alert.setHeaderText(null);
530	    alert.setContentText(message);
531	    alert.showAndWait();
532	}
533	
534	private void showInfo(String message) {
535	    Alert alert = new Alert(AlertType.INFORMATION);
536	    alert.setTitle("Information");
537	    alert.setHeaderText(null);
538	    alert.setContentText(message);
539	    alert.showAndWait();
540	}
541	```
542	
543	## UI Components
544	
545	### StepWizard
546	
547	`fan.summer.api.component.StepWizard` is a ready-made multi-step wizard container. Use it for any tool that follows a step-by-step workflow (e.g., file selection, configuration, execution).
548	
549	```java
550	import fan.summer.api.component.StepWizard;
551	
552	// Create the wizard
553	StepWizard wizard = new StepWizard();
554	
555	// Add steps: title, content node, validation supplier
556	wizard.addStep("Select file",  fileSelectNode, () -> filePath != null);
557	wizard.addStep("Split mode",   modeSelectNode, () -> modeSelected);
558	wizard.addStep("Run split",    progressNode,   () -> true);
559	
560	// Required: call build() after all addStep() calls
561	wizard.build();
562	
563	// Listen for step changes (e.g., trigger analysis on forward)
564	wizard.setOnStepChanged((from, to, total) -> {
565	    if (from == 0 && to == 1) {
566	        startAnalysis();
567	    }
568	});
569	
570	// Programmatic navigation
571	wizard.goTo(2);
572	boolean isLast = wizard.isLastStep();
573	```
574	
575	**Behavior**:
576	
577	- Renders step dots with done/active/idle visual states
578	- Animated slide transitions between steps (with direction awareness)
579	- Back/Next buttons in the footer
580	- The `canProceed` supplier (third argument to `addStep`) is evaluated on every Next click
581	- Returning `false` triggers a shake animation on the Next button and blocks advancement
582	- On the last step, the Next button text changes to "Complete"
583	
584	### Layout Recommendations
585	
586	Use JavaFX layout panes:
587	
588	| Pane | Use For |
589	|------|---------|
590	| `VBox` | Vertical stacking with spacing |
591	| `HBox` | Horizontal row of elements |
592	| `BorderPane` | Top/center/bottom/left/right layout |
593	| `GridPane` | Grid-based forms |
594	| `StackPane` | Overlapping elements, centering |
595	| `FlowPane` | Wrapping flow layout |
596	| `ScrollPane` | Scrollable content |
597	
598	```java
599	VBox root = new VBox(12);
600	root.setPadding(new Insets(20));
601	root.setAlignment(Pos.TOP_LEFT);
602	
603	Label heading = new Label("Excel Splitter");
604	heading.getStyleClass().add("section-title");
605	
606	// Content fills remaining space
607	VBox.setVgrow(contentArea, Priority.ALWAYS);
608	root.getChildren().addAll(heading, contentArea);
609	```
610	
611	## Theming
612	
613	SwissKit uses a three-layer CSS architecture with a glassmorphism dark theme:
614	
615	| File | Module | Scope |
616	|------|--------|-------|
617	| `css/swisskit-common.css` | `SwissKitJ-Api` | Shared variables, scrollbars, progress bar, `.glass-*` utility classes, `.section-title`/`.section-header` |
618	| `css/shell.css` | `SwissKit` | App shell only: `.titlebar`, `.sidebar`, `.search-bar`, `.tool-card`, `.detail-panel`, `.statusbar` |
619	| `css/builtin.css` | `SwissKit` | Built-in tool styling (reserved) |
620	
621	### For Plugins Embedded in the Main Scene
622	
623	Plugins whose `createView()` returns a Node that the host embeds in the main Scene automatically inherit all three stylesheets via scene graph propagation. No action needed.
624	
625	### For Plugins with Their Own Stage/Scene
626	
627	If your plugin opens its own window, apply the common stylesheet:
628	
629	```java
630	import fan.summer.api.theme.Themes;
631	
632	Stage popup = new Stage();
633	Scene scene = new Scene(root);
634	Themes.applyTo(scene);  // Loads shared glass-* utility classes
635	popup.setScene(scene);
636	popup.show();
637	```
638	
639	### Available CSS Classes (from swisskit-common.css)
640	
641	| Class | Purpose |
642	|-------|---------|
643	| `.glass-dialog` | Dialog/popup background style |
644	| `.glass-field` | Text field style |
645	| `.glass-combo` | Combo box style |
646	| `.glass-table` | Table view style |
647	| `.glass-checkbox` | Checkbox style |
648	| `.glass-btn-primary` | Primary action button |
649	| `.glass-btn-secondary` | Secondary action button |
650	| `.glass-tab-pane` | Tab pane style |
651	| `.section-title` | Section heading text |
652	| `.section-header` | Section header container |
653	
654	## Logging
655	
656	Plugins should use `fan.summer.api.log.LoggerFactory` rather than depending on any logging framework directly.
657	
658	```java
659	import fan.summer.api.log.LoggerFactory;
660	import fan.summer.api.log.PluginLogger;
661	
662	public class MyPlugin implements SwissKitJPlugin {
663	    private static final PluginLogger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MyPlugin.class);
664	
665	    @Override
666	    public void onActivate() {
667	        log.info("Activated, taskId={}", currentTaskId);
668	    }
669	
670	    private void processFile(Path file) {
671	        try {
672	            log.debug("Processing file: {}", file);
673	            // ...
674	            log.info("File processed successfully: {} ({} rows)", file.getName(), rowCount);
675	        } catch (Exception e) {
676	            log.error("Failed to process file: {}", file, e);
677	        }
678	    }
679	}
680	```
681	
682	**Logging levels**: `trace`, `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error` -- all with SLF4J-style `{}` placeholder support.
683	
684	**Backend**: The host application installs a binder at startup that routes plugin log calls through SLF4J + Logback:
685	- Console output at INFO+ level
686	- Rolling file at DEBUG+ level under `.swisskit/logs/swisskit.log`
687	- Daily rotation with 7-day retention
688	
689	**Unit testing**: If the host has not installed a binder (e.g., during plugin unit tests), `LoggerFactory` returns a silent no-op logger, so it is always safe to call.
690

722	## Building
723	
724	### Clean Build
725

728	mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests
729	mvn clean package -DskipTests
730	```
731	
732	### Skip Tests
733	
734	```bash
735	mvn clean package -DskipTests
736	```
737	
738	### Build Only Main App
739	
740	```bash
741	mvn clean package -pl SwissKit -am -DskipTests
742	```
743	
744	### Create and Run Fat JAR
745	
746	```bash
747	# Build
748	mvn clean package -DskipTests
749	
750	# Run
751	java -jar SwissKit/target/SwissKit-3.0.0-alpha.1.jar
752	```
753	
754	### Windows Executable
755	
756	On Windows, the `windows-exe` Maven profile is auto-activated and produces `SwissKit.exe` via Launch4j in `SwissKit/target/`.
757	
758	## Common Tasks
759	
760	### Adding a Dependency
761	
762	Edit the relevant `pom.xml`:
763	
764	```xml
765	<dependency>
766	    <groupId>com.example</groupId>
767	    <artifactId>library-name</artifactId>
768	    <version>1.0.0</version>
769	</dependency>
770	```
771	
772	Then run:
773	
774	```bash
775	mvn dependency:resolve
776	```
777	
778	### Debugging
779	
780	Run the application with debug mode in your IDE:
781	
782	1. Set breakpoints in your code
783	2. Run `fan.summer.Launcher.main()` in debug mode
784	
785	For verbose logging, the host already routes logs to `.swisskit/logs/swisskit.log` at DEBUG level. Plugin logs are automatically included.
786	
787	### Platform-Specific Notes
788	
789	- **Linux**: JavaFX media dependencies may require `libglib2.0-0`, `libgtk-3-0`, and other system libraries
790	- **macOS**: JavaFX works out of the box with JDK 21+
791	- **Windows**: The `windows-exe` profile auto-activates, producing an `.exe` via Launch4j
792	
793	## Best Practices
794	
795	### Thread Safety
796	
797	- Always update UI components on the JavaFX Application Thread
798	- Use `Platform.runLater()` for UI updates from background threads
799	- Use `javafx.concurrent.Task` for long-running operations -- its `updateMessage()` and `updateProgress()` methods are thread-safe
800	
801	```java
802	// Wrong -- from background thread
803	statusLabel.setText("Done");
804	
805	// Correct
806	Platform.runLater(() -> statusLabel.setText("Done"));
807	
808	// Even better -- use Task
809	task.updateMessage("Processing completed");
810	```
811	
812	### Error Handling
813	
814	- Always handle exceptions gracefully
815	- Provide user-friendly error messages via JavaFX `Alert`
816	- Log errors for debugging
817	
818	```java
819	try {
820	    processFile(inputPath);
821	} catch (Exception e) {
822	    log.error("File processing failed", e);
823	    Platform.runLater(() -> {
824	        Alert alert = new Alert(AlertType.ERROR);
825	        alert.setTitle("Error");
826	        alert.setHeaderText("Processing Failed");
827	        alert.setContentText(e.getMessage());
828	        alert.showAndWait();
829	    });
830	}
831	```
832	
833	### Resource Management
834	
835	- Use try-with-resources for file and I/O operations
836	- Release resources in `onUnload()` (thread executors, watchers, DB connections)
837	- Unbind JavaFX properties when a task completes
838	
839	```java
840	try (Workbook workbook = WorkbookFactory.create(file)) {
841	    // Use workbook
842	} // Automatically closed
843	
844	@Override
845	public void onUnload() {
846	    executorService.shutdownNow();
847	    watcher.close();
848	}
849	```
850	
851	### Plugin Isolation
852	
853	- Keep plugin dependencies self-contained in a fat JAR
854	- Do not assume the host application's classpath contains your dependencies
855	- Test your plugin JAR in an isolated environment before distribution
856	
857	## Contributing
858	
859	See the [Contributing Guide](contributing.md) for guidelines on contributing to SwissKit.
